description_long: This object is a compact assault rifle with a black metal body and wooden accents on the handguard and pistol grip. The rifle features a detachable curved magazine, which is shown removed and lying next to several loose rifle cartridges. The stock is a metal folding type, designed for portability and ease of storage, and the barrel is relatively short, making the weapon suitable for close-quarters urban environments. The rifle is equipped with standard iron sights for aiming, and the muzzle appears to have no additional device attached. The overall design is reminiscent of the AKS-74U, a variant of the Kalashnikov family, known for its reliability and rugged construction. The visible surfaces are mostly matte black metal with some glossy wooden parts, and the object is detailed with functional components such as the trigger, magazine release, and charging handle.
description: A compact black and wood assault rifle with a folding metal stock, detachable curved magazine, and standard iron sights. The magazine and several cartridges are displayed separately beside the weapon.
description_view_0: Front view showing the muzzle, front sight, and the magazine and cartridges placed in front of the rifle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full profile of the rifle, the wooden handguard and grip, the folding stock, and the detached magazine with cartridges.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the stock, pistol grip, and the ejection port area, with the magazine and cartridges visible to the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the rifle, the folding stock, and the separated magazine and cartridges.
